selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend a few times monthly on social media

messages.content:
Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.","persona":{"age":63,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"heterosexual","occupation":"retail worker","city_of_living":"Naples","employment_status":"employed full-time","education_level":"vocational diploma","parents_education":{"parent_1":"lower secondary","parent_2":"upper secondary"},"current_marital_status":"married","children":2,"migration_status":"native-born Italian","psychological_feature":"depressed","religious_beliefs":"Islam","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend a few times monthly on social media","hobbies":["reading novels","hiking","classical music"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":49,"level":"moderate"},"conscientiousness":{"score":85,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":70,"level":"high"},"agreeableness":{"score":39,"level":"moderate"},"neuroticism":{"score":84,"level":"high"}}},"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

mode = human
topic = How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
persona = {"age":63,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"heterosexual","occupation":"retail worker","city_of_living":"Naples","employment_status":"employed full-time","education_level":"vocational diploma","parents_education":{"parent_1":"lower secondary","parent_2":"upper secondary"},"current_marital_status":"married","children":2,"migration_status":"native-born Italian","psychological_feature":"depressed","religious_beliefs":"Islam","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend a few times monthly on social media","hobbies":["reading novels","hiking","classical music"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":49,"level":"moderate"},"conscientiousness":{"score":85,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":70,"level":"high"},"agreeableness":{"score":39,"level":"moderate"},"neuroticism":{"score":84,"level":"high"}}}

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.",
  "opinion": "As a middle-aged man from Naples, I've found these kinds of talks quite challenging. My first reaction is always the same: it's not something I can handle easily given my depression and how things are in life right now. But I do have an approach to deal with them. First off, I think you need to be clear-headed about what you're talking about, even if your head feels fuzzy most of the time. My high conscientiousness helps me a bit there, but it's not enough alone. For instance, when someone starts chatting about COVID vaccines online, I take a deep breath because my neuroticism kicks in and makes me anxious easily. Then I try to think like this: have you got any sources? Because that's what my agreeableness score tells me – I want polite discussions without shouting matches, especially on social media where I spend only occasional times but still get stressed out by comments.",
  "reasoning_summary": "Start with assessing the need for clarity in understanding the topic due to depression and moderate openness. Then use high conscientiousness and agreeableness traits to approach topics logically while maintaining politeness despite neuroticism increasing anxiety levels. Finally, consider how religious beliefs might influence perspectives but remain open enough through moderate openness.",
  "tone": "Calm but slightly worried",
  "citations": ["Personal experience from retail work in Naples", "General knowledge of healthcare systems"],
  "safety_notes": ""
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion: As a middle-aged man from Naples, I've found these kinds of talks quite challenging. My first reaction is always the same: it's not something I can handle easily given my depression and how things are in life right now. But I do have an approach to deal with them. First off, I think you need to be clear-headed about what you're talking about, even if your head feels fuzzy most of the time. My high conscientiousness helps me a bit there, but it's not enough alone. For instance, when someone starts chatting about COVID vaccines online, I take a deep breath because my neuroticism kicks in and makes me anxious easily. Then I try to think like this: have you got any sources? Because that's what my agreeableness score tells me – I want polite discussions without shouting matches, especially on social media where I spend only occasional times but still get stressed out by comments.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Start with assessing the need for clarity in understanding the topic due to depression and moderate openness. Then use high conscientiousness and agreeableness traits to approach topics logically while maintaining politeness despite neuroticism increasing anxiety levels. Finally, consider how religious beliefs might influence perspectives but remain open enough through moderate openness.
citations: Personal experience from retail work in Naples
